question:
Problem B-1
Let \( n \) be a positive integer, and define
\[
f(n)=1!+2!+\cdots+n!
\]

Find polynomials \( P(x) \) and \( Q(x) \) such that
\[
f(n+2)=P(n) f(n+1)+Q(n) f(n)
\]
for all \( n \geqslant 1 \).
solution:
B-1.
We have
\[
f(n+2)-f(n+1)=(n+2)!=(n+2)(n+1)!=(n+2)[f(n+1)-f(n)] .
\]

It follows that we can take \( P(x)=x+3 \) and \( Q(x)=-x-2 \).

descriptive_long.question:
Problem B-1
Let \( indexvar \) be a positive integer, and define
\[
sumfactor(indexvar)=1!+2!+\cdots+indexvar!
\]

Find polynomials \( polyfirst(inputvar) \) and \( polysecond(inputvar) \) such that
\[
sumfactor(indexvar+2)=polyfirst(indexvar) sumfactor(indexvar+1)+polysecond(indexvar) sumfactor(indexvar)
\]
for all \( indexvar \geqslant 1 \).
descriptive_long.solution:
We have
\[
sumfactor(indexvar+2)-sumfactor(indexvar+1)=(indexvar+2)!=(indexvar+2)(indexvar+1)!=(indexvar+2)[sumfactor(indexvar+1)-sumfactor(indexvar)] .
\]

It follows that we can take \( polyfirst(inputvar)=inputvar+3 \) and \( polysecond(inputvar)=-inputvar-2 \).

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
Problem B-1
Let \( longitude \) be a positive integer, and define
\[
cylinder(longitude)=1!+2!+\cdots+longitude!
\]

Find polynomials \( sandwich(photograph) \) and \( backpack(photograph) \) such that
\[
cylinder(longitude+2)=sandwich(longitude) cylinder(longitude+1)+backpack(longitude) cylinder(longitude)
\]
for all \( longitude \geqslant 1 \).
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
B-1.
We have
\[
cylinder(longitude+2)-cylinder(longitude+1)=(longitude+2)!=(longitude+2)(longitude+1)!=(longitude+2)[cylinder(longitude+1)-cylinder(longitude)] .
\]

It follows that we can take \( sandwich(photograph)=photograph+3 \) and \( backpack(photograph)=-photograph-2 \).

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
Problem B-1
Let \( negativeindex \) be a positive integer, and define
\[
fixedvalue(negativeindex)=1!+2!+\cdots+negativeindex!
\]

Find polynomials \( nonpolynomial(constantvalue) \) and \( irrational(constantvalue) \) such that
\[
fixedvalue(negativeindex+2)=nonpolynomial(negativeindex) fixedvalue(negativeindex+1)+irrational(negativeindex) fixedvalue(negativeindex)
\]
for all \( negativeindex \geqslant 1 \).
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
B-1.
We have
\[
fixedvalue(negativeindex+2)-fixedvalue(negativeindex+1)=(negativeindex+2)!=(negativeindex+2)(negativeindex+1)!=(negativeindex+2)[fixedvalue(negativeindex+1)-fixedvalue(negativeindex)] .
\]

It follows that we can take \( nonpolynomial(constantvalue)=constantvalue+3 \) and \( irrational(constantvalue)=-constantvalue-2 \).

garbled_string.question:
Problem B-1
Let \( qzxwvtnp \) be a positive integer, and define
\[
bvxrtkwe(qzxwvtnp)=1!+2!+\cdots+qzxwvtnp!
\]

Find polynomials \( sdlkfjwe(hjgrksla) \) and \( aowpeiqu(hjgrksla) \) such that
\[
bvxrtkwe(qzxwvtnp+2)=sdlkfjwe(qzxwvtnp) bvxrtkwe(qzxwvtnp+1)+aowpeiqu(qzxwvtnp) bvxrtkwe(qzxwvtnp)
\]
for all \( qzxwvtnp \geqslant 1 \).
garbled_string.solution:
B-1.
We have
\[
bvxrtkwe(qzxwvtnp+2)-bvxrtkwe(qzxwvtnp+1)=(qzxwvtnp+2)!=(qzxwvtnp+2)(qzxwvtnp+1)!=(qzxwvtnp+2)[bvxrtkwe(qzxwvtnp+1)-bvxrtkwe(qzxwvtnp)] .
\]

It follows that we can take \( sdlkfjwe(hjgrksla)=hjgrksla+3 \) and \( aowpeiqu(hjgrksla)=-hjgrksla-2 \).

kernel_variant.question:
Let $n$ be a non-negative integer and define
\[
f(n)=0!+1!+2!+\dots +n!.
\]
Find polynomials $P(x)$ and $Q(x)$ such that
\[
f(n+3)=P(n)\,f(n+2)+Q(n)\,f(n+1)
\]
holds for every $n\ge 0$.
kernel_variant.solution:
First note that for every n\geq 0
\[f(n+3)-f(n+2)=(n+3)!\]
Using the factorial identity (n+3)!=(n+3)(n+2)! we obtain
\[f(n+3)-f(n+2)=(n+3)(n+2)!\]
Because f(n+2)-f(n+1)=(n+2)!, we may substitute (n+2)! = f(n+2)-f(n+1) to get
\[f(n+3)-f(n+2)=(n+3)\bigl[f(n+2)-f(n+1)\bigr]\]
Rearranging gives
\[f(n+3)=(n+3)f(n+2)-(n+3)f(n+1)+f(n+2)
      =(n+4)f(n+2)-(n+3)f(n+1).\]
Therefore the required polynomials are
\[P(x)=x+4\quad\text{and}\quad Q(x)=-(x+3),\]
which indeed satisfy
\[f(n+3)=(n+4)f(n+2)-(n+3)f(n+1)\qquad(n\geq 0).\]
This completes the proof.

core_steps: Write the forward difference  f(n+2) − f(n+1)  as the new summand  (n+2)!.
Substitute and collect terms to obtain  f(n+2) = (n+3)f(n+1) − (n+2)f(n).

slot1.description: Fixed forward step in the target recurrence  f(n+Δ) = …  (currently Δ = 2).  Any other positive integer step would admit the same ‘difference-of-sums’ argument repeated Δ times.

slot2.description: Lower limit of the defining sum for  f(n) (now starts at 1!).  Shifting to 0! or any other constant starting index leaves the difference method unchanged.
